title: Amazon S3 outage (Feb 2017)
text: Amazon S3 in US-EAST-1 experienced widespread outages that cascaded to many dependent services. Symptom: elevated error rates and inability to access objects across the region. Root cause: during debugging of a billing subsystem, one of the commands intended to remove a small number of servers was issued with a typo, which inadvertently removed a much larger set of servers, including capacity for two core S3 subsystems (the index subsystem and the placement subsystem). Removing too much capacity forced a full restart of these subsystems. Fix/mitigation: the affected subsystems required a full restart and integrity checks before returning to service; Amazon added safeguards to the removal tool to prevent capacity from being taken below a minimum required level and to remove capacity more slowly.

title: Azure leap-year certificate outage (Feb 2012)
text: Windows Azure suffered a global outage lasting most of a day. Symptom: cloud services failed to initialize, cascading across clusters. Root cause: the code that generated transfer certificates computed a validity date as the current date plus one year. On February 29, 2012 (a leap day) this produced an invalid expiration date of February 29, 2013, which does not exist; certificate creation failed, guest agents could not initialize, and the health system misinterpreted the failures and spread the problem to more servers. Fix/mitigation: correct the date calculation to handle leap years, and add limits so that the health system does not aggressively spread failures across clusters.

title: GitHub DNS outage (2014)
text: GitHub experienced a DNS-related disruption after changing an IP address. Symptom: name resolution problems following an IP change. Root cause: a Puppet manifest bug restarted only the authoritative nameserver but not the caching nameserver, leaving stale or inconsistent DNS state. Fix/mitigation: improved the provisioning API and added handling for zone file corruption so that both nameserver roles are restarted consistently.

title: CircleCI PostgreSQL schema outage (Nov 2021)
text: CircleCI jobs became stuck in a not-running state. Symptom: builds queued but never executed. Root cause: a deploy changed the type of a field in the PostgreSQL database, and strict schema validation in the application failed against the new type, so the service could not process jobs. Fix/mitigation: hand-deployed a build that ignored the offending field and manually scaled the affected components to drain the backlog, then corrected the schema change process.

title: Cloudflare BGP outage (June 2022)
text: Cloudflare had a malfunction affecting 19 of its busiest datacenters. Symptom: traffic was dropped in affected locations during a network topology change. Root cause: as part of a long-running project to increase resilience, a change to the ordering of BGP advertised prefixes was applied incorrectly; the incorrect ordering of the disabled prefixes withdrew routes that should have stayed up. Fix/mitigation: correct the prefix ordering in the configuration and roll back the offending change across affected datacenters.

title: Allegro traffic-spike outage (2018)
text: The Allegro e-commerce site went down after a marketing campaign drove a sudden traffic spike. Symptom: the site became unavailable under load. Root cause: a configuration error in cluster resource management prevented additional service instances from starting, so the system could not scale out to absorb the spike. Fix/mitigation: restore the resource configuration to allow dynamic scaling and add headroom/limits review to campaign readiness.

title: Google Compute Engine BGP outage (2014)
text: Google Compute Engine suffered widespread connectivity outages. Symptom: instances became unreachable. Root cause: a bad autogenerated configuration removed all Google Compute Engine IP blocks from BGP announcements, so the network stopped advertising routes to GCE. Fix/mitigation: restore the correct BGP configuration and add validation for autogenerated network configs.

title: Amazon DynamoDB DNS race condition outage (Oct 2025)
text: On October 19-20, 2025, a DynamoDB outage in US-EAST-1 cascaded to EC2 instance launches, Lambda invocations, Fargate and many other services for roughly 15 hours. Symptom: elevated errors reaching the regional DynamoDB endpoint (dynamodb.us-east-1.amazonaws.com), which resolved to an empty DNS record. Root cause: a latent race condition in the DynamoDB DNS management system; one DNS Enactor was delayed while applying an older plan, a second Enactor applied a newer plan and triggered cleanup of old plans, then the delayed Enactor overwrote the newer plan with its stale older plan, and the cleanup process deleted that active plan, removing all IP addresses for the regional endpoint and leaving the system unable to apply further updates automatically. Fix/mitigation: AWS disabled the DNS Planner/Enactor automation worldwide, and committed to fixing the race condition and adding protections against applying incorrect DNS plans before re-enabling it.

title: Heroku configuration propagation outage (2016)
text: Heroku web dynos could not be started. Symptom: applications failed to boot new dynos. Root cause: an automated remote configuration change did not propagate fully to all components, leaving them in an inconsistent state. Fix/mitigation: improve the configuration distribution system to ensure changes propagate atomically and completely.

title: GitHub ZooKeeper split-brain (Oct 2020)
text: GitHub saw about 10% of background-job writes fail over 2 hours and 32 minutes. Symptom: intermittent write failures for background jobs. Root cause: replacement ZooKeeper hosts were added too quickly, which elected a second leader and created two distinct ZooKeeper clusters (a split-brain), so writes went to inconsistent quorums. Fix/mitigation: adopt a sequential host addition protocol so replacement nodes join one at a time and a single leader is maintained.

title: Stack Overflow regex ReDoS (July 2016)
text: Stack Overflow had a service outage triggered by health-check failures. Symptom: servers were marked unhealthy and removed from rotation. Root cause: the backtracking implementation in the underlying regular expression engine became extremely expensive for one particular post whose text triggered catastrophic backtracking, consuming CPU until health checks timed out. Fix/mitigation: optimize or bound the regex handling for the problematic pattern and add safeguards against catastrophic backtracking.

title: Strava primary key overflow (2014)
text: Strava experienced upload failures. Symptom: new uploads were rejected. Root cause: a database table hit the signed integer limit on its primary key, so no new rows with auto-incrementing IDs could be inserted. Fix/mitigation: perform a schema migration to a larger integer data type (e.g., bigint) for the primary key.

title: Stripe manual operation outage (2015)
text: The Stripe API was down for about 90 minutes. Symptom: API requests failed. Root cause: a manual operation on the production database was done incorrectly, missing a dependency required for the change to be safe. Fix/mitigation: add enhanced procedure checklists and validation for manual production database operations to prevent missing dependencies.

title: Twilio Redis billing incident (2013)
text: Twilio issued multiple erroneous charges to customers. Symptom: customers were billed incorrectly and repeatedly. Root cause: a network partition in the Redis cluster caused the master to crash and restart in read-only mode; billing writes failed and a retry loop repeatedly re-attempted charges, producing duplicate charges. Fix/mitigation: enhance transaction handling to be idempotent and add monitoring for Redis partition/read-only states.

title: Basecamp integer limit outage (Nov 2018)
text: Basecamp was stuck in read-only mode. Symptom: users could read but not write. Root cause: a database table hit the integer limit on an ID column, so new writes with auto-incremented IDs could not be inserted, forcing the system into read-only mode. Fix/mitigation: migrate the affected column to a larger integer type (bigint) to restore write capability.

title: Reddit Kubernetes upgrade outage (March 2023)
text: Reddit had an outage of more than 5 hours. Symptom: site-wide unavailability during a maintenance window. Root cause: a critical Kubernetes cluster upgrade failed because node metadata (specifically a label) changed format between Kubernetes versions, which broke the pod networking (Calico route reflector selection) and brought down workload networking. Fix/mitigation: roll back the upgrade and add pre-upgrade compatibility validation for node metadata and networking components.

title: incident.io Postgres PGAudit lock (Jan 2025)
text: incident.io had its dashboard and API unavailable for about 2 minutes. Symptom: brief but total API/dashboard unavailability. Root cause: the PGAudit extension hung while holding critical locks and ignored timeout signals, which blocked database operations. Fix/mitigation: remove the PGAudit extension entirely to eliminate the locking behavior.

title: CrowdStrike Falcon global BSOD (July 2024)
text: A CrowdStrike Falcon content update caused Windows machines worldwide to blue-screen (BSOD), affecting about 8.5 million machines. Symptom: mass Windows crashes on boot. Root cause: a content configuration update contained undetected errors that caused an out-of-bounds memory read in the sensor's kernel-mode component, crashing the OS. Fix/mitigation: revert the faulty content (done within 78 minutes) and improve the content validator plus staged/canary rollout for content updates.
